item.title: Week 4
item.description: Pretty. I like the edges of the gray clouds a lot. They seem out of place? But in such a way that they serve only to better the view. :) A blob holds an axe! I wonder what they are slashing. I really like the mix of colors around the sun in these remaining photos. I thought it looked super cool! :DDD

item.title: Day 28
item.description: To-do Dailies [ ] Read Folland for 5 minutes & try problems for 5 minutes [ ] Read Make it Stick for 5 minutes [ ] Read Tao Complex for 5 minutes [ ] Read Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Sea for 5 minutes [ ] Look at graduate school stuff for 30 minutes

item.title: Day 27
item.description: To-do Dailies [ ] Read Folland for 5 minutes & try problems for 5 minutes [ ] Read Make it Stick for 5 minutes [ ] Read Tao Complex for 5 minutes [ ] Read Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Sea for 5 minutes [ ] Look at graduate school stuff for 30 minutes Thoughts I should be more realistic about my goals and what I intend to do. It’s good to aim high but if you aim straight up you just hit yourself in the head again.

item.title: Week 3
item.description: I guess I didn’t take any photos of clouds since the last post? Kinda funky.

item.title: Day 18
item.description: To-do Today [X] Drive home! [X] Just enjoy your day back home. :) Future & Deadlines [ ] Send honors counseling a message about the incomplete Thoughts So happy to be back home. Things to think about I can do it. I can. I have to make that stick!

item.title: Day 17
item.description: To-do Today [X] Finish moving stuff into new apartment [X] Do some chores before leaving [X] Get ready to leave at 6:00 AM! Thoughts Home!

item.title: Week 2
item.description: I liked the tree and stuff. I honestly have no recollection of where this is? Maybe near frat row? This was near the hospital. OH I realize that the previous photo was also here, just a different direction! :) Wilshire and the urban hellscape that is Los Angeles has some modicum of beauty among its development, for once. /rolls-eyes I thought this was a nice color so I zoomed in!

item.title: Closing My Eyes
item.description: I close my eyes often. Beyond blinking I choose to close them to imagine what has been, is, and can be. I used to close my eyes and wish to open them to a new world, To see the light of hope in another life, To see all of this through eyes which were not my own. Recently when I close my eyes I imagine what can be — what I can be.

item.title: Day 8
item.description: To-do Today [X] Study for Galois Theory [X] Work on Complex Analysis Homework [ ] Work on Differential Forms Homework 4 [ ] Study for Persian final! item.title: Week 1
item.description: I took this photo when walking with home with my good friend Matt. :) I think that was the last time I will see him at UCLA as he’s going to Caltech now for his teaching/post-doc job. I took the the remaining photos while walking from my friend Jacob’s performance of “The Waitress”! It was amazing. One of my absolute favorite plays that I’ve seen. :) He did a fantastic job!

item.title: May 2023
item.description: Westwood Boulevard might have blocked some of the sky but what remained was pretty darn nice. :) This reminds me of my high school fish-bowl sky photo! On the corner of Gayley and Lindbrook. Those monolithic, empheral, white mountains above the construction site brings some much needed beauty lol. These IFOs (Identified Flying Objects) are quite convincing! (They have been identified to be the lights in my friend’s apartment.)

item.title: Pre-College
item.description: Here are my favorite clouds from my high school years. I took this photo before my zero-period P.E. class in my sophomore year of high school. It was my favorite photo that I’d ever taken at that time. :) God’s white udders fill the sky with its blue milk. I can’t identify this color because I’m incredibly colorblind but it still looks really cool. It looked like I was in a fish bowl when I took this photo.

item.title: Day 2
item.description: To-do Today [ ] Study for Galois Theory [X] Get swiffer, brita, kettle, etc. for apartment Future (by date) [ ] <Jun. 3> Prep for Complex Analyis midterm on Monday! [ ] <Jun. 4> Do Complex Analysis homework [ ] <Jun. 4> Resubmit Differential Forms homework 2 [ ] <Jun. 4> Resubmit Differential Forms homework 3 [ ] <Jun. 7> Complex Analysis homework due Thoughts Today was also very overwhelming. I don’t know if I can do it, but I know I have to at least try, so I’m going to keep trying.

item.title: Day 1
item.description: To-do [x] Exterminator appointment [X] Study for Galois Theory [ ] Look at Complex Analysis homework [ ] Look into resubmitting Differential Forms homework [X] Study for Farsi quiz tomorrow - Past/Present subjunctive, imperative, past/present perfect, and past progressive tenses. Thoughts I’ve been feeling a bit overwhelmed with how this quarter has gone, but it’ll be okay. I don’t know what precisely will happen but I will not let whatever does happen overwhelm me further.

item.title: Of Mountains and Shirts
item.description: The mountain is immovable. It stands strongly despite all it endures. Its clothes may be ragged but it continues to do what it must. May I be a mountain. May my shirt have holes.

item.title: about me
item.description: me! Hello! My name is Mealaud Mokhtarzad but I usually go by Mel. I’m a pure math major at UCLA (class of ’24). My interests include and are usually limited to: math, rock skipping, clouds, and writing. If you need to contact me, m.mokhtarzad at ucla dot edu is usually a good way to reach me. Cool People Hoober Loo – Hunter is a lovely, epic friend I met in our graduate complex analysis class.

item.title: Atlas: The Dive
item.description: I am unburdened. I am unburdened not because I have surmounted the seemingly-insurmountable, but because I have forgotten the weight I bear. It seems impossible that I could forget to carry my life, my responsibilities, my hopes, my dreams, but with a lack of clarity, things that may seem at the forefront of your mind may disappear into this all-too-powerful fog. I lost focus. I forget. I forget what I enjoyed and what I despised.

item.title: Atlas
item.description: I crumple. The world, my world, is the weight I bear. My responsibilities, my decisions good and bad, my hopes and dreams, everything I consider myself to be—the product of my very own existence—is held up by me. I strain trying to stay upright but my ankles falter. I nearly fall but I catch myself. As the world begins to slip out of my hands I tighten my grip and attempt to stand but my knees buckle and the greatest, tallest stand I can take is an imperfect squat—I am unbalanced.

item.title: The Mountains' Shirts Have Holes
item.description: The mountains rest. They sleep peacefully, happily, unstirringly night and day. They rest as the window blows and bellows across. The trees leave them be. They dig their roots into their skin, into their flesh, but they don’t mind. The trees, in becoming part of them, turn into their clothes. The imperfect canopy reveals their rocky skin. Their shirts have holes.

item.title: The Mountains Dance
item.description: The wind whistles and howls, It runs by the surface of our hills terrorizing the grass below. In their desperation, Attempting to avoid devastation, They dodge. Back and forth they sway, side to side. They say, They yell, “We will not relent! “The misers may be strong but we are more!” They continue to further themselves. Spreading their roots, Planting themselves firmly into their histories, They grow stronger, The wind blows.

item.title: The Tempest
item.description: The tempest erupts once again and everything is re-enlivened. The beings of the old calibrate to the changing times, their roots rearrange and change, their leaves vibrate in the unrelenting winds, while their trunks stand strong in the force of powers greater than itself. The tree that guards its underlings curls over to these winds. He forces the direction of the curl to move in a way such that his children are protected.

item.title: Dawn to Noon
item.description: He rises. The knights, peacefully resting, wait for their next beckoning. They hold their breath as their king rises over the foothills, inching towards his kingdom’s highest point. In his divine righteousness, he spreads rays of golden hope, promising a new day free from the strains of the last. Invigorated, his knights turn to bundles of fire and flames, transforming into a perfect inferno of red and orange. Their enchanting aggression lays siege to the blue plains, expanding the territory in which their kingdom lies.

item.title: Cloud Watching
item.description: I feel the damp, soft grass push lightly against my back as I watch the puffy clouds transform above. I stare and I see a beautiful, fluffy, poorly drawn rabbit. He doesn’t seem to move much. The wind bellows and leaves ruffle around me as my homely rabbit morphs into something a little more exciting. Fangs erupt from his mouth, his body elongates and curves, his button tail is grabbed and pulled by a gust, while his back is blown into curved wings.
